summary: Zed Shaw shares a brief progress update on his second cast drawing, depicting an old man he believes is a saint. He humorously dubs the piece 'Saint Anger' after the Metallica album he listened to while working. The drawing is done in pencil on Ampersand clay board. The post is a simple visual progress log with minimal commentary.
key_points: Shaw is practicing classical cast drawing techniques as part of his art education
He brings his characteristic humor to art practice, naming the piece after Metallica's album
The work demonstrates his commitment to traditional media (pencil on clay board)
money_quotes: I call him Saint Anger because I listened to mostly Metalica while I worked on it.
key_insight: Shaw's art practice follows traditional atelier methods like cast drawing, approached with his signature irreverent humor.
